key_insight: HiReLC uses hierarchical reinforcement learning to jointly prune and quantize neural networks, combining per-block low-level decisions with global budget coordination from high-level agents.
engineering_relevance: The approach offers an architecture-agnostic path to compressing large models like ViTs and CNNs while reducing storage and preserving accuracy.
method: Low-level agents choose per-block compression actions: bitwidth, keep-ratio, quantization type, and granularity.
High-level agents allocate compression budgets using ensemble voting and Fisher Information-based sensitivity estimates.
An active-learning loop uses an MLP surrogate and a logit-MSE proxy to reduce expensive policy evaluations during search.
evidence_signal: Across Vision Transformer and CNN benchmarks, the method reports roughly 5.99–6.72× storage compression with accuracy changes ranging from a 3.83% gain to 0.55–5.62% drops.
watch_item: The surrogate helps with reward shaping, but final gains still depend on costly post-compression evaluation and fine-tuning, so search efficiency may be sensitive to implementation details.
paper_title: Hierarchical Reinforcement Learning for Neural Network Compression (HiReLC): Pruning and Quantization
